overview.Summary: A complex cyber attack targeting a healthcare organization, involving multiple stages including reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Stealing sensitive patient data and disrupting healthcare operations.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather information about the target organization's structure, employees, and potential vulnerabilities using publicly available sources.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers develop a simple credential harvesting tool using Python for basic functionality.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers craft phishing emails with malicious links or attachments to distribute to the target organization.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers set up a free or low-cost web hosting service to host malicious payloads.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers deliver malicious attachments via phishing emails to compromise user credentials.

stage.name: Execution
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.description: The attackers execute the malicious payload once the user interacts with the phishing link or attachment.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers establish persistence by adding a registry entry or startup folder item to ensure the payload runs on subsequent reboots.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers use known vulnerabilities in exposed applications to escalate privileges.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers terminate or uninstall antivirus/endpoint protection to avoid detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers capture credentials from memory or extract them from storage to enable lateral movement.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers map the internal network to identify all connected devices and services.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attackers use WMI or PSExec to remotely execute commands on other systems within the network.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers search for and collect sensitive patient health information (PHI) or financial data.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers encrypt the stolen data before transferring it to an external server for safe retrieval.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers delete or alter critical data to disrupt healthcare operations and create chaos.

Scenario.summary: A sophisticated cyber attack campaign targeting a healthcare organization, leveraging multiple stages to infiltrate, persist, escalate privileges, evade defenses, steal credentials, discover internal resources, move laterally, collect sensitive data, exfiltrate it securely, and ultimately destroy critical information to disrupt operations.
